title: Sabarimala case: 10 arguments by Centre before Supreme Court which could shape India’s religious laws
description: The Union government has filed its written submissions before the 9-judge Constitution Bench of the Supreme Court in the Sabarimala reference.The written submissions filed through Solicitor General Tushar Mehta has asked the Supreme Court to set a lasting judicial policy on how constitutional courts approach religious disputes, handle Public Interest Litigations (PILs), and interpret the freedom of religion.The submissions urge a shift away from recent judicial trends, advocating for a stricter
